reasoning: The fetch_ts tool outputted a baseline probability anchor of 0.41, factoring in a negative short-term trend and weak seasonality for this time of year. Because EIX is a utility paying a high dividend (expecting ~$0.83 quarterly, with upcoming ex-dividend dates projected around early October 2025 and early January 2026), the stock will experience mechanical downward adjustments of its price over the 3-month and 6-month horizons. This further creates a headwind against the nominal price exceeding the August 31, 2025 baseline without resolving the massive wildfire liability uncertainties. As the forecasting horizon grows, standard expanding variance swamps the mean drift, slowly pulling probabilities back toward the random-walk 0.50 baseline.
